items.title: Meet the Calgarian behind Team Canada's World Cup 2026 jerseys
items.summary_auto: Team Canada's 2026 kit has been in the works since the last World Cup four years ago —  and it's the brain child of a Calgarian. As the lead designer at Nike for the national team's latest kit, Calgary-based Holly Gallacher was behind the designs of the national team's home and away jerseys.

items.title: Carney announcing decision on future of 24 Sussex in coming weeks: sources
items.summary_auto: Prime minister Mark Carney is expected in the coming weeks to announce the government's decision about the future of 24 Sussex Drive, sources tell CBC News.

items.title: Toronto gang war led police to a trove of evidence in Ryan Wedding cocaine case, court docs say
items.summary_auto: More than two years after the arrest of alleged hitman Malik Damion Cunningham in a Toronto suburb, new details about the circumstances of the police operation have emerged from unsealed warrant documents. Cunningham was purportedly hired, armed and trained by Ryan Wedding’s alleged drug ring.

items.title: N.L. Crown prosecutor charged with smuggling, prohibited weapon possession
items.summary_auto: The RCMP in eastern Newfoundland have charged a provincial Crown attorney with more than 10 charges related to smuggling, illegal importation, and weapons offences.

items.title: 'No need for panic' on Ebola case in France, WHO chief says
items.summary_auto: WHO director generalTedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, speaking on Wednesday about the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, weighed in on the report of a recent case of Ebola in a doctor in France who had worked in Congo, saying the risk to the rest of the world remains low.

items.title: Aurora, Ont., to remove Frank Stronach's name from recreation complex after sexual assault conviction
items.summary_auto: The Town of Aurora will remove Frank Stronach's name from its Aurora Recreation Complex after the 93-year-old Canadian billionaire was found guilty of one count of sexual assault and one count of indecent assault at his trial last week.

items.title: PQ maps out its vision for an independent Quebec in Blue Book on sovereignty
items.summary_auto: Quebec independence would yield $13 billion to $16 billion in administrative savings by eliminating or merging most of the roughly 230 federal agencies operating in the province, Parti Québécois (PQ) Leader Paul St-Pierre Plamondon announced Tuesday.

items.title: Weight-loss drug approved to treat sleep apnea in Canada
items.summary_auto: Health Canada has approved an injection medication to treat severe obstructive sleep apnea in adults with obesity.

items.title: Hamilton city council approves pause on data centre development
items.summary_auto: City of Hamilton staff will report back to councillors at their next meeting on July 15 with a bylaw imposing a moratorium on the construction of the facilities that securely store, process and distribute digital information.

items.title: What we learned from Bill Gates's interview with Congress about Epstein
items.summary_auto: Microsoft founder and philanthropist Bill Gates told a congressional panel that files released by the Justice Department in January only added to his embarrassment over interacting with the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Does it seem easier to book a dermatologist for Botox than a mole check? You're not alone
Woman killed in Israeli strike in Lebanon remembered as defender of endangered sea turtles
items.summary_auto: Mona Khalil, who turned her beachfront home in southern Lebanon into a conservation hub for endangered sea turtles, succumbed to her wounds from an Israeli strike on her home.

items.title: As major U.S. Supreme Court decisions loom, how far will it let Trump go?
items.summary_auto: America’s top court is set to deliver decisions on more than a dozen major cases in the coming days. And with rulings on birthright citizenship, transgender athletes and the Federal Reserve on the docket, there’s a lot at stake as the U.S. president’s agenda is tested by the justice system.

items.title: 2 victims of Côte-des-Neiges shooting being honoured today in ceremonies
items.summary_auto: The two victims killed in Monday's Côte-des-Neiges shooting — a Montreal police officer and a civilian — are being honoured at two separate ceremonies on Wednesday.

items.title: 2 victims of Montreal shooting being honoured today in ceremonies
items.summary_auto: The two victims killed in Monday's Côte-des-Neiges shooting — a Montreal police officer and a civilian — are being honoured at two separate ceremonies on Wednesday.

items.title: Feds push to have 3 northern projects fast-tracked under Building Canada Act
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government wants a Northwest Territories highway, an Arctic deepwater port and a nuclear waste storage facility in northwestern Ontario to be listed as projects of national interest.

items.title: N.B. man found guilty of 1st-degree murder in stabbing deaths of boy, teen
items.summary_auto: A Saint John man has been found guilty of two counts of first-degree murder in the stabbing deaths of two boys, aged 10 and 17, last year.

items.title: Michigan Senate candidate accuses Trump of keeping Canada-U.S. bridge closed to help donor
items.summary_auto: Mallory McMorrow's new ad accuses Trump of blocking the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge and suggests he is doing it to benefit a major political donor, building on an anti-corruption message she has sought to make central to her campaign.

items.title: Toronto saw significant increase in speeders after camera removals: city report
items.summary_auto: In a new report Mayor Olivia Chow describes as "horrifying," Toronto transportation staff say there has been a significant increase in speeding where cameras used to be located in the city.

items.title: Landmark legal win compensates pregnant soccer player who lost contract
items.summary_auto: In a landmark legal win for female soccer players, sport's highest court has awarded Maja Göthberg salary compensation from Lazio Women after the Italian club ended contract talks when it knew she was pregnant.

items.title: Deadly 'Omega' heat wave breaks more temperature records in Europe
items.summary_auto: Western Europe was in the grip of a heat wave on Wednesday that claimed dozens of lives, disrupted power supplies, shut schools and cultural landmarks, as forecasters warned the extreme temperatures could persist until the end of the week.

items.title: Love Story or Hoax? All of the Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding rumours
items.summary_auto: It’s a date likely etched into the minds of many Swifties: Aug. 26, 2025 — the day Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce announced their engagement on Instagram. Since then, fans have been fixated on uncovering possible details about when and where the wedding may take place.

items.title: Sask. veterinarian recruits chatty sheep to be blind llama's bestie
items.summary_auto: A Saskatchewan veterinarian went on a search to find her newly blind llama named Maple, a companion to help her adjust to her new life. That new companion came in the form of Peeps the sheep.

items.title: Ukraine launches drone offensive in Crimea and inside Russia, where fuel shortages are taking a toll
items.summary_auto: Ukrainian drones knocked out power in the biggest city in Russian-held Crimea on Wednesday and targeted facilities ‌in central and southern Russia, underscoring the reach of Kyiv's attacks on energy infrastructure.

items.title: 'The man who was loved by all': Funeral service honours Toronto police officer killed during investigation
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of mourners gathered in Etobicoke Wednesday afternoon to honour Const. Marc Pinizzotto, who was fatally shot during a police raid earlier this month.

items.title: Study examines how many YouTubers offering nutrition advice are actually qualified to give it
items.summary_auto: Registered dietitian Leah Cahill says people come to her all the time asking about nutrition information they've seen on social media. She is one of the authors of a new study looking at the quality of that influencer advice.

items.title: Abuse alleged at N.L. preschool as parents demand answers
items.summary_auto: Parents of children who attended a pre-kindergarten program in eastern Newfoundland say their kids were knelt on, yelled at, and locked in a bathroom by an early childhood educator who police investigated but never charged.

items.title: Why a 25% discount on airfare cost a Montreal couple more — thanks to dynamic pricing
items.summary_auto: A Montreal couple was confused and upset after they rebooked their Air Canada flight to take advantage of a new sale, and the rebooked tickets cost slightly more. Dynamic pricing, which can cause base fares to fluctuate, was partly to blame. Some experts are calling for more transparency when companies use dynamic pricing during a discount promotion.

items.title: Orillia, Ont., to decide fate of controversial Samuel de Champlain statue
items.summary_auto: As the central Ontario city of Orillia decides what will happen to an imposing, century-old statue of Samuel de Champlain, it remains hidden away in storage. The city council will choose to either restore it, modify it, send it away or melt it down.

items.title: StubHub cancels thousands of World Cup tickets, leaving fans furious and heartbroken
items.summary_auto: StubHub is facing thousands of angry consumer complaints after cancelling World Cup tickets hours before game time.

items.title: Trump threatened new tolls in the Strait of Hormuz. What if other countries follow suit?
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to impose new tolls in the Strait of Hormuz last weekend, sparking worries that the practice of charging to pass through critical waterways could spread — with devastating consequences to the global economy. Iran has blocked the strait for much of the war, reportedly charging tolls as high as $2 million US for ships it allows to pass.

items.title: India’s Cockroach Party shows frustrated Gen-Zers are hard to crush
items.summary_auto: India’s Cockroach Janta Party has extended its protest in Delhi to a fourth day, as it demands the resignation of the country's education minister. The CJP, which emerged just over a month ago, is aimed at India’s bulging youth demographic, which is frustrated over a lack of jobs and a test scandal in education.

items.title: Canada's AI strategy aims to make health data more accessible. How will it protect privacy?
items.summary_auto: Connecting health data across the country will help researchers conduct clinical trials, test AI health tools and drive innovation in treatment and diagnosis, says federal Minister of Artificial Intelligence Evan Solomon. But the initiative will also have to balance privacy considerations.

items.title: Israel committed genocide in Gaza, deliberately targeted Palestinian children: UN report
items.summary_auto: The UN's Commission of Inquiry found that Israel had genocidal intent in the Gaza Strip during the war, with the key indicator being its targeting of children. The Gaza Health ministry says that since the ceasefire began in October 2025, more than 1,000 people died, 265 of them children.

items.title: Immigration minister says 'something' happened to trigger order to surrender citizenship docs
items.summary_auto: Immigration Minister Lena Diab said she told her department to investigate when she became aware that "something" had happened to trigger a wave of citizenship document recalls.

items.title: Years in the making: An inside look at the PWHL's 1st outside investment
items.summary_auto: A "belief in the growth of the game" is behind a "significant" investment in the PWHL from the Detroit-based Ilitch Companies, according to a company executive. The Ilitch Companies and Toronto businessman Larry Tanenbaum's Kilmer Sports Ventures were announced as the first outside investors in the PWHL earlier this week.
